title: galium glaucum var. hirsutum
intro: Galium glaucum var. hirsutum, also known as Long-stalked Bedstraw or Hairy Bedstraw, is a plant species within the Rubiaceae family, which is native to Europe and Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The plant's leaves and stems are said to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and treating skin conditions. Traditionally, herbal preparations of Galium glaucum var. hirsutum include infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which are used to treat a range of health issues, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
